concerns.text: T013 allows a 'regex-based fallback' if RAM > 7GB. This silently weakens FR-002 which mandates the system 'MUST utilize a CPU-tractable small language model'. A regex fallback is not a language model. The task must enforce the model requirement or explicitly document the fallback as a scope change in the spec, not just the task.

T014 requires generating a rule library with '≥90% coverage'. However, there is no preceding task to measure, validate, or enforce this threshold. T013 generates the rules, but no task exists to calculate the coverage metric against the validation split. This drops the acceptance criterion from FR-002.

SC-001 requires a 'paired t-test (or Wilcoxon signed-rank test)' for 'Time-to-Pivot' differences. T025 only implements 'mixed-effects logistic regression' (for Success Rate, per FR-006). T029 is a generic 'Generate final report' task. No task explicitly implements the t-test/Wilcoxon logic required by SC-001.

T021 creates a script to run the baseline on 'external resources'. FR-004 mandates the system 'MUST execute... the full AutoResearchClaw baseline agent (on standard resources: 4 cores, 16 GB RAM)'. While the plan notes external execution, the task description 'external resources' is vague and risks violating the 'identical resource constraints' logic required for the comparison if the external environment isn't strictly defined as 4c/16GB.

FR-005 requires metrics to be 'stratified by the annotated failure feature type'. T020 only says 'records... for every task'. T023 handles the stratification logic, but T020's deliverable (the recording step) is ambiguous about whether the stratification is embedded in the record or applied later. This risks dropping the 'stratified' acceptance criterion if T023 is skipped or fails.
